mentions.note: Down badly today (down 4 bucks); Cramer sees dip-buy opportunity. Buy a little on weakness, if it dips more buy more. Long-term holder.

Up only 9% in 2025 despite strong fundamentals. Beaten down early on growth, tariff fears (China, India). Strong quarters, iPhone 17 success, avoided AI data center capex arms race. Down $9 from early December highs. Trading 32x forward earnings (not cheap but deserved given quality). Could land huge AI platform default deal (maybe Gemini). Own it, don't trade it for Investing Club.
January rebalancing causing selling as money managers raise cash for new positions; fundamentals unchanged, company humming along profitably. Own it, don't trade it.
Despite down today on expensive phone concerns, Taiwan Semi transcript shows strong demand; stock has done nothing but poised to outperform.
Consumer electronics and tech; essential growth holding; major diversifier.
Down 9% YTD, facing 8th consecutive week of declines; being liquidated to fund speculative names; take profits on any bounce.
Memory cost inflation major drag on margins; rising chip costs one reason Apple underperforming; supply-chain cost pressure.
Mentioned as long-term winner in Magnificent 7 / Fang+ portfolio; facing multiple compression and investor rotation to storage plays but Cramer maintains conviction on mega-cap tech reflow.
Down 8 straight weeks; other side of storage shortage trade — needs to pay sky-high storage prices, margin pressure. Own it, don't trade it; if guides margins down, decline may not be over.

Own it, don't trade it. 8-week losing streak but up $7.37 today on memory margin commentary. Watch gross margins, iPhone 17 durability, Gemini partnership ROI, and Siri reboot progress Thursday. Dodges massive CapEx rivals are burning.
Great momentum but trades at one of highest P/E multiples in entire market. Too much danger from valuation; Cramer avoiding despite company quality.
iPhone 17 sales $85B vs. $79B consensus; robust top/bottom beat; own it, don't trade it; monitor memory price headwinds.

Terrific quarter with 22% iPhone growth, America strength, first China growth in ages; bearish chip-cost thesis overblown; Tim Cook unusually bullish suggesting margin confidence; supply-chain mastery, customer power, and carrier subsidies protect pricing; finished +$120 despite midday weakness.
Pessimists starting to tremble; terrific guidance last week despite bears controlling narrative. Momentum shifting; potential move ahead.
Blowout iPhone sales, China strong, gross margin guidance solid; avoided capex via Google Gemini partnership; up ~8% since quarter; own, don't trade; holding for Travel Trust.
One of two conviction stocks with own-it-don't-trade-it mentality in portfolio; despite painful days (down heavily Feb 27), philosophy has made money; hold through volatility.
Foldable phone delay rumor (Nikkei Asia) debunked by Bloomberg; stock fell 13 points on false story, rallied 5 on correction; own don't trade; will 'keep flying higher' if no war tomorrow.
Eric's portfolio: core holding; own it, don't trade it.
Never really went anywhere; Cramer thinks ready to roll; Mag 7 recovery narrative intact.
Huge beneficiary of AI revolution; Tim Cook's leadership resulted in $4T valuation with 1,932% gain; John Turnus named CEO designate, strong succession. Own for long-term AI upside.
Tim Cook built 1,900% return via unwavering consumer focus and integrity; no compromise on quality; 1.5B users see device as friend—rarest CEO achievement.
Own it, don't trade it. Tim Cook handing to John Turnus (effective Sept 1). Exceptional momentum; new iPhones, foldables, iPhone 17 line keep going higher.
Reports Thursday. Huge user base lets it leverage Google Search and Gemini without massive capex spend. Different model than hyperscalers.
Reports Thursday night after Big Four on Wednesday; turning point for earnings season; market peaked before recent mega-cap tech clusters.
Revenue +17%, guidance good; no controversy despite iPhone questions; own it don't trade it; camera excellence (17 Pro Max) shows manufacturing mastery; should have popped more post-earnings.
One of only two stocks blessed with 'own it, don't trade it' status due to revolutionary products and outstanding management execution; must continuously do homework to ensure thesis remains intact.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Consumer brand so obvious it's hiding in plain sight; sustained pricing power; services expansion; rate-proof fundamentals.
Best-of-breed with loyal customer base, growing service revenue, and fortress balance sheet. At $23 in 2016 with low P/E it was a fabulous entry despite persistent skeptics.
Best hardware combined with Gemini integration via Google search deal; smart Siri getting smarter. No near-term AI anxiety warranted; stock near all-time highs on execution discipline.

Doesn't need to raise capital for AI; piggybacking on Alphabet's massive investment. With 2.5B active users, ecosystem too valuable for competitors to ignore; Siri AI upgrade at WWDC should be transformative.
WWDC reaction overstated by traders; Siri/Gemini integration is real value driver. 2.5B device base justifies AI partnership premium. One of few megacap winners left—don't lose the leader.
Still has fantastic balance sheet, buyback, and beloved products. Will recover as market rotates from pure growth to quality and profitability.
Own it, don't trade it; current selling is speculators raising SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI IPO cash, not fundamental deterioration. Long-term core hold with Alphabet Gemini deal intact.
Raising phone prices $200 to pass on memory chip cost inflation from hyperscaler demand. Consumer electronics getting expensive; could become national political issue if trend continues.
